title: Within-protein (2-field-invisible) non-coding biophysical variance is substantial at Class I HLA loci (29–69% at HLA-A; Z-penalty 68.9%) and corresponds to expression-variant alleles at d up to 2.49

subject.display: Migration cohort for 'Within-protein (2-field-invisible) non-coding biophysical variance is substantia'
subject.note: Inclusion predicates intentionally empty at migration time; the implicit member set is defined by the v1.1 premises. Re-author with explicit inclusion predicates to tighten the subject.

source.note: 4-field IPD-IMGT/HLA alleles grouped by 2-field protein (first two numeric fields). Exp26 variance decomposition restricts to protein groups with >=2 four-field alleles: HLA-A 162 groups / 2604 alleles, HLA-B 255/3307, HLA-C 178/3078, HLA-DRB1 69/515.

premises.note: 9504 = sum of per-locus allele counts above.

source.note: IMGT suffix annotations: normal, L (low), Q (questionable), S (secreted). N (null) excluded from the within-protein expression-variant test because null is a coding-level disruption, not a biophysical effect.
Per-allele whole-allele biophysical metrics including CpG density, NC stacking ΔG₃₇, GC, total Z-form penalty, CpG island count.

operations.note: Random-intercept model per locus × metric. Output: (% between-protein variance, % within-protein variance). The within-protein component is invisible to 2-field matching.
Cohen's d within each qualifying protein group between normal and expression-variant alleles. 56 of 11,879 protein groups qualify across 6 loci. Reports per-locus max |d| and mean |d|.

statistics.name: hla_a_within_protein_variance_percent_range
statistics.note: HLA-A whole-allele variance partition (Table 5). Range 28.2–68.9% within-protein.

statistics.name: hla_a_max_within_protein_variance_pct
statistics.note: Max over HLA-A metrics is Z-form penalty at 68.9%.

statistics.name: hla_a_min_within_protein_variance_pct
statistics.note: Min over HLA-A metrics is CpG density at 29.0%.

statistics.name: hla_a_top_within_protein_cohens_d
statistics.note: Max |Cohen's d| across 12 qualifying HLA-A protein groups. Achieved by A*01:01 on CpG density (228 normal alleles vs 1 L-expression variant). The load-bearing 'non-coding biophysics differs substantially within protein-identical alleles' stat.

statistics.name: hla_a_mean_within_protein_cohens_d
statistics.note: Mean |d| across 12 qualifying HLA-A protein groups. Directional consistency: 67% of groups show variant > normal CpG density.

statistics.name: drb1_within_protein_variance_percent_range
statistics.note: HLA-DRB1 whole-allele variance partition — Class II contrast. Within-protein variance <10% across metrics, meaning DRB1 non-coding divergence tracks coding lineage tightly (opposite of Class I).

statistics.name: drb1_max_within_protein_variance_pct
statistics.note: Max over DRB1 metrics is CpG island count at 9.6% — still below Class I minima.

inference.justification: Four conjuncts license the 'within-protein biophysical layer is real and matching-invisible' claim: (1) HLA-A minimum within-protein variance > 20% establishes that even the most between-protein-dominated metric still has substantial within-protein signal; (2) HLA-A max > 50% establishes that some metrics (Z-penalty) are majority within-protein — those features are essentially invisible to 2-field matching; (3) top Cohen's d > 2.0 confirms the within-protein biophysical variation corresponds to actual expression-variant phenotypes, not just structural noise; (4) DRB1 max < HLA-A min (stat-vs-stat) establishes the Class I / Class II contrast — DRB1's tight coupling of coding and non-coding divergence is a meaningful internal control that makes the Class I story structural rather than generic.
inference.failure_mode: If HLA-A within-protein variance fell below 20% universally, the 'hidden layer' framing collapses to edge-case noise. If d_top dropped below 1.0, the within-protein variation would be biologically silent — an interesting structural decomposition with no functional consequence. If DRB1 also showed 29–69% within-protein variance, the Class I specificity would dissolve into a locus-invariant pattern and we couldn't separate signal from pipeline artifact.

conclusion.assertion: At Class I HLA loci, 29–69% of non-coding biophysical variance resides within 2-field protein groups (HLA-A Z-form penalty: 68.9%), and the within-protein variation corresponds to actual expression-variant alleles with Cohen's d up to 2.49 (A*01:01 CpG density, 228 normal vs 1 low-expression allele). The pattern is Class I specific: HLA-DRB1 shows <10% within-protein variance across all metrics, indicating non-coding divergence at Class II loci is structured by protein lineage rather than accumulating independently. Current 2-field transplant matching is therefore blind to substantial, expression-relevant non-coding biophysical variation at Class I.

scope.scale_note: Whole-allele scope at 4-field resolution. The DPB1*11:01 d=25.44 outlier on Z-penalty is noted in the manuscript but deliberately excluded from the load-bearing inference conjuncts (single-allele observation).

confidence.note: Per-metric within-protein percentages from random-intercept lme4 fit; Cohen's d from within-group t-test. Bootstrap CIs staged but not pinned here.

external_assumptions.statement: Hierarchical variance partitioning with protein-group random intercepts faithfully separates between-protein and within-protein components when at least 2 alleles per protein group are available.

IMGT expression-suffix annotations (normal / L / Q / S) are a valid (if coarse) proxy for within-protein expression-variant phenotype; null-suffix alleles are excluded because null is a coding-level effect.
The Class I / Class II contrast (within-protein variance substantial at Class I, <10% at DRB1) is a genuine biological pattern, not a pipeline artifact of sample-size differences across loci.
